channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fremantle Stages Late Comeback to Beat Hawthorn Despite Gunston’s Strong Start
item.description: Hawthorn forward Jack Gunston looked set to dominate against Fremantle, kicking five of six goals in the first half and giving the Hawks a 22-point lead midway through the third term. Nick Watson also...

item.title: Argentina Investigates Hantavirus Outbreak Linked to Cruise Ship
item.description: Argentine authorities are urgently investigating whether the country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has affected passengers on a cruise ship en route to Antarctica. The outbreak ha...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Resumes Proceedings in Pretoria
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, tasked with investigating allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, is continuing its procee...

item.title: Racing tips and previews for Saturday at Gosford
item.description: The Canberra Times provides an extensive preview and betting guide for Saturday racing at Gosford. Key highlights include Priory Park, who is tipped to be hard to beat in Race 1 after a strong debut a...

item.title: European Workers Show High Job Market Optimism but Low Workplace Engagement
item.description: In 2025, European employees remain highly optimistic about local job opportunities, with 57% indicating it is a good time to find work—a level that has held steady since 2024 and represents the larges...

item.title: IGP orders all police officers to wear name tags on uniform for accountability
item.description: The Inspector General of Police, Tunji Disu, has ordered all police officers across Nigeria to always wear name tags on their uniforms to make them easily identifiable. He said this is to ensure accou...

item.title: A curated selection of political cartoons published on 8 May 2026
item.description: This Sydney Morning Herald entry presents a curated gallery of political cartoons published on 8 May 2026. Rather than a traditional written article, the piece is structured as a visual collection fea...

item.title: Man convicted of manslaughter after fatal altercation outside Leicestershire pub
item.description: A 37-year-old man, Nathan Gothard, has been found guilty of manslaughter for the death of 66-year-old David Darke following an altercation outside The Crown Inn in Appleby Magna, Leicestershire, just ...

item.title: Zinc phosphide from rat poison confirmed as cause of death of Mumbai family
item.description: In a tragic incident in South Mumbai last month, four members of a Muslim family – husband, wife, and their two daughters – died following suspected food poisoning. Initially, watermelon consumption w...

item.title: Note from Jeffrey Epstein’s suspected suicide attempt released by U.S. judge
item.description: A note purportedly found by Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate after the financier’s suspected suicide attempt in July 2019 has been unsealed and released by a U.S. District Judge in New York. The shor...

item.title: Teenager faces murder charge following fatal Woolwich shooting
item.description: Authorities in Woolwich have charged a second teenager in connection with the tragic shooting of 14-year-old Eghosa Ogbebor. The incident, which has deeply shocked the local community, occurred recent...

item.title: Johannesburg shoppers brave winter queues for Stella McCartney H&M collection
item.description: On a chilly Johannesburg morning, shoppers lined up outside H&M Sandton well before sunrise to get their hands on the Stella McCartney collaboration. Temperatures hovered around 5°C as customers wrapp...

item.title: Abuja Court Convicts Ex-Power Minister Saleh Mamman on 12 Counts of Money Laundering
item.description: A Federal High Court for Abuja don convict former Minister of Power, Saleh Mamman, on all 12 counts wey EFCC put for ground, based on money laundering allegations involving N33.8 billion meant for Zun...

item.title: Fred Matiang'i Image Circulated Online Was Altered, Not Asleep During Interview
item.description: Recently, a doctored image of former Kenyan Interior Cabinet Secretary and presidential aspirant Fred Matiang'i went viral on social media, claiming he was asleep during a live interview on Citizen TV...

item.title: India urges Bangladesh to expedite nationality verification; TVK seeks support in Tamil Nadu
item.description: India has emphasised the need for Bangladesh to accelerate the nationality verification process for undocumented Bangladeshi migrants so that their repatriation can be completed smoothly. Ministry of ...

item.title: How Digital Technology Shapes Our Thoughts and Decisions
item.description: In the digital age, the way we think, learn, and interact is increasingly influenced by technology. Mahnoor Jillani reflects on how social media and AI can subtly shape opinions and behaviours. From p...

item.title: TMC MP Mahua Moitra Reports Harassment on IndiGo Flight to Delhi, Seeks Airline Action
item.description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra has alleged that she experienced harassment from a group of men on board an IndiGo flight 6E 719 while travelling to Delhi for official work. The incident reportedly...

item.title: AI Models Can Sometimes Give False Information, Users Should Verify Facts
item.description: Recent developments in artificial intelligence have highlighted a concerning trend: AI systems, including ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Anthropic, and Grok, can sometimes generate information that is factu...

item.title: Lethbridge Council Delays Decision on Integrated Fire and EMS Services Funding
item.description: Lethbridge city council in Alberta has postponed a decision on funding its integrated fire and Emergency Medical Services (EMS). Earlier this year, the provincial government asked the city to either i...

item.title: Breathe Pakistan 2026 highlights urgent climate action and inclusive policymaking
item.description: The second Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference, organised by DawnMedia, wrapped up in Islamabad after two days of in-depth discussions on climate resilience, adaptation, and susta...
